title: Rafnia gibba
taxonomy: Rafnia gibba is classified within the family Fabaceae, specifically within the subfamily Faboideae, and is further classified as belonging to the genus Rafnia. Rafnia gibba is a member of the tribe Crotalarieae, which is a group of flowering plants that share certain characteristics. The plant Rafnia gibba is a succulent species native to southern Africa. The classification of Rafnia gibba is based on its morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics. Rafnia gibba's classification is also supported by its close relationships with other species within the family Fabaceae.

intro: Rafnia gibba, also known as the hairy rafnia or hairy Rafnia, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in South African folk medicine for its various health benefits, which include treating fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. Rafnia gibba contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Rafnia gibba is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
